title: Zinriss "Book" Numeri
summary: A polished exile-bookkeeper whose ruined accounts and social graces pull him into Alkenstar's hidden financial web.
concept: Cultivated exile bookkeeper entangled in suspicious debts and the city's money trails.
partyRole: social connector / financial analysis / respectable-front access

background.origin: Anvilstone, from the Numeri tailor family on his father's side and the Sharian line on his mother's.
background.history: Zinriss once kept the books for Numeri Skraedderi but found the work stifling and drifted into helping around the local area. A bookkeeping disaster left him owing far more than he could pay, and he fled to Alkenstar rather than become trapped in a dwarven family contract. In the city he survives through manners, languages, presentation, and financial competence, while maintaining ties to Garik Geode, PapirHer, Romano Letuuse, and other respectable-seeming contacts.
background.incitingIncident: A bookkeeping mistake left him with crushing debt and forced him to flee to Alkenstar.
background.reasonInCity: To survive exile, build leverage through trade and account work, and understand the money networks that now surround him.

currentSituation.overview: Living rough but presentably in Alkenstar, he uses polish, pipe smoke, and careful social ties to stay useful.
currentSituation.legalStatus: Fled debt-related fallout in Anvilstone; exact formal legal status in Alkenstar is unclear.
currentSituation.socialStatus: Well dressed and well mannered, but more connected than genuinely trusted.
currentSituation.currentProblem: His old accounting scandal still shadows him, and his place in Alkenstar's financial web may drag him deeper into Mugland-linked trade.

goals.shortTerm: Stabilize his position in Alkenstar and turn his financial skill into protection and leverage.
goals.midTerm: Learn who truly benefited from the bookkeeping disaster and whether he was set up or merely used.
goals.longTerm: Clear his name and expose or outmaneuver the corrupt financial network behind the city's trade and weapons economy.

spotlight.gmNotes: Bring Book forward in scenes about ledgers, trade routes, social access, debt pressure, and the respectable surface of corrupt commerce.

relationshipNotes.label: Garik Geode
relationshipNotes.note: A dwarf companion and pipe-smoking veranda contact.

relationshipNotes.label: PapirHer
relationshipNotes.note: Automaton helper tied to his current life in the city.

relationshipNotes.label: Romano Letuuse
relationshipNotes.note: Goblin owner of Vino Babino and part of Book's cultivated network.

specialCapabilities: Financial reading of people and institutions

campaignFitSummary: Strongly tied to Alkenstar's money flows, respectable fronts, and the hidden economics behind Mugland's network. Best used in debt, trade, ledger, and social-maneuver scenes.
notes: Built from partial freeform notes. Whether Zinriss was framed or simply made an honest but catastrophic mistake remains socially contested; source framing leans toward innocence.

relations.note: Landlord, friend, and relaxed social anchor in Alkenstar.
His skills and contacts make him a natural entry point into the city's hidden account books.
